title: Spacetime singularities & their quantum-gravity resolution
statement: General relativity predicts that gravitational collapse produces a spacetime singularity where curvature invariants diverge and the classical theory breaks down. The Penrose-Hawking theorems prove such singularities are generic (not artifacts of symmetry), given reasonable energy and causality conditions. What physically replaces the singularity is unknown and is believed to require a quantum theory of gravity, which does not yet exist in complete, predictive form.

originators.name: Karl Schwarzschild
originators.note: 1916 vacuum solution exhibiting the r=0 curvature singularity (and the coordinate singularity at r=2M).

originators.name: Roger Penrose
originators.note: 1965 singularity theorem using trapped surfaces and global methods; Nobel Prize 2020.

originators.name: Stephen Hawking
originators.note: extended the theorems (with Penrose) to cosmology and generic collapse.

ranking.rationale: The deepest and oldest: resolving it requires the missing theory of quantum gravity. Ranked #1 because no other black-hole problem is more fundamental or has resisted longer.

key_ideas: Curvature singularities are where geodesics are incomplete and invariants like the Kretschmann scalar diverge.
Penrose's trapped-surface argument makes singularity formation generic, independent of exact symmetry.
Cosmic censorship (problems #04, #05) concerns whether such singularities are hidden behind horizons.
Candidate resolutions replace r=0 with a quantum-gravity core: a bounce (loop quantum gravity), a fuzzball/string condensate, or a regular (non-singular) black-hole interior.
Any resolution must reduce to classical GR in the appropriate limit and respect the singularity theorems' assumptions or evade one of them (e.g. via quantum energy-condition violation).

approaches.name: Loop quantum gravity / quantum bounce
approaches.summary: Discrete quantum geometry replaces the singularity with a bounce to an expanding region.

approaches.name: String theory / fuzzballs
approaches.summary: The interior is a horizon-scale quantum bound state of strings/branes with no singular core.

approaches.name: Regular (non-singular) black holes
approaches.summary: Effective metrics (Bardeen, Hayward) with a de Sitter core; phenomenological, seeking a fundamental basis.

approaches.name: Asymptotic safety / other QG programs
approaches.summary: Running couplings soften the UV behavior of gravity near the core.

papers.title: On the Gravitational Field of a Mass Point According to Einstein's Theory
papers.note: Sitzungsber. Preuss. Akad. Wiss.; the Schwarzschild solution.

papers.title: Gravitational Collapse and Space-Time Singularities
papers.note: Phys. Rev. Lett. 14, 57; the first modern singularity theorem (trapped surfaces).

papers.title: The Singularities of Gravitational Collapse and Cosmology
The Large Scale Structure of Space-Time
papers.note: Cambridge University Press; definitive treatment of the singularity theorems.

papers.title: Quantum Nature of the Big Bang

physicists.name: Roger Penrose
physicists.contribution: Singularity theorems via trapped surfaces and global causal methods; Nobel Prize 2020.

physicists.name: Stephen Hawking
physicists.contribution: Singularity theorems in cosmology and generic collapse.

physicists.name: Abhay Ashtekar
physicists.contribution: Loop quantum gravity; quantum bounce replacing classical singularities.

physicists.name: Samir Mathur
physicists.contribution: Fuzzball proposal for a non-singular black-hole interior.

physicists.name: Demetrios Christodoulou
physicists.contribution: Rigorous results on collapse and trapped-surface formation.

simulator_angle: A classical-GR ray tracer cannot probe the singularity, but it can map the trapped region / horizon structure and visualize geodesic incompleteness up to the horizon; regular-black-hole metrics are a drop-in alternative metric to render and compare against Kerr.

metadata.coverage_note: Seed: 5 canonical references (bibliographic notes; arXiv ids to be verified) + 5 key physicists. Aspiration is up to 25 papers / 10 physicists, to be expanded by sourced enrichment.
